mission.title: Install and Configure Argo CD Operator
mission.description: The Argo CD Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to manage Argo CD clusters, enabling GitOps workflows in Kubernetes environments. It simplifies the deployment and management of Argo CD instances, making it easier to integrate continuous delivery practices. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in managing operator lifecycle events.

steps.title: Add the Argo CD Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Argo CD Operator Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the operator.

```bash

helm repo add argocd-operator https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the repository for the Argo CD Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Argo CD Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace argocd-operator

helm install argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd-operator --version 0.12.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Argo CD Operator.
> Installs the Argo CD Operator in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Create the CatalogSource for the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This step creates a CatalogSource resource that points to the Argo CD Operator image registry.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

```

> Creates the CatalogSource resource for the operator.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

steps.title: Create the OperatorGroup
steps.description:
This step creates an OperatorGroup resource to define the scope of the operator's permissions.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
EOF

```

> Creates the OperatorGroup resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
EOF

steps.title: Create the Subscription for the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This step creates a Subscription resource to manage the lifecycle of the Argo CD Operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

```

> Creates the Subscription resource for the operator.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Argo CD Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Argo CD Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if any Argo CD instances are still using the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall argocd-operator --namespace argocd-operator

kubectl delete namespace argocd-operator

```

> Uninstalls the Argo CD Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Argo CD Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Argo CD configurations. This step will upgrade the operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get argocd -n argocd-operator -o yaml > argocd-backup.yaml

helm upgrade argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd-operator --version 0.12.0

kubectl get pods -n argocd-operator

```

> Backup the current Argo CD configuration.
> Upgrades the Argo CD Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the Argo CD Operator pods are running.
commands: kubectl get argocd -n argocd-operator -o yaml > argocd-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "CatalogSource" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OpenShift Operator Lifecycle Manager (OLM) is not installed or not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure that OLM is installed and running in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create subscription: catalog source not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CatalogSource resource was not created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the CatalogSource resource and ensure it is correctly defined and applied.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Subscription" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OLM is not installed or the API version is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify that OLM is installed and that you are using the correct API version.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in the argocd-operator namespace are in a CrashLoopBackOff state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the operator's configuration or the underlying Kubernetes resources.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the operator pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n argocd-operator' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-cluster management and improved RBAC configurations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy API versions for CatalogSource and Subscription.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.11.0, ensure that your existing resources are compatible with the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Argo CD Operator running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n argocd-operator' returning a status of 'Running' for all pods.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
